title: Render ammeter transient current graphs in AnalogSimulationViewer
description: Fixes the issue where ammeter current waveforms were not rendered in the AnalogSimulationViewer despite being generated during simulation.
body: ## Summary `AnalogSimulationViewer` rendered simulation results by collecting only `simulation_transient_voltage_graph` IDs and forwarding them to `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSimulationSvg`. Any `simulation_transient_current_graph` produced by an `` was silently dropped, so ammeter current waveforms never appeared in the viewer even though the simulation generated them. ## Changes - Collect `simulation_transient_current_graph_id`s from the circuit JSON alongside the existing voltage-graph IDs. - Pass `simulation_transient_current_graph_ids` through to `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSimulationSvg` so current waveforms are rendered into the schematic simulation SVG. - Rename `simulationGraphIds` -> `simulationVoltageGraphIds` to make the voltage vs. current distinction explicit, and include both in the `useMemo` dependency arrays so the SVG regenerates when either changes. ## Reproduction Added `examples/example22-ammeter-current-probe.fixture.tsx`: a voltage source feeding a resistor through an ammeter (`AM1`), with a `VOUT` voltage probe. Before this change the ammeter's current graph was absent from the rendered simulation; after it, both the current and voltage waveforms render. ## Before / After | | Voltage graphs | Current graphs | |---|---|---| | Before | rendered | dropped | | After | rendered | rendered |

title: Fix duplicate same-net labels from schematic trace fallback insertion
description: Fixes the issue of duplicate overlapping schematic net labels for named nets by reusing existing labels instead of inserting duplicates.
body:
### Motivation

Named nets such as `BAT` could render duplicate overlapping schematic net labels near the same port. This happened in the fallback logic for ports missing a visible schematic trace: when a nearby label already represented the same source connection, the fallback did not safely reuse it for non-ground nets, so another label could be inserted for the same net.

In `repro132`, this showed up around `R8_BAT_TOP`, where `BAT` label could overlap or appear visually detached from the connected port.

### Change

- Reuse a nearby same-net schematic label instead of inserting a duplicate.
- Re-anchor an existing same-text label to the current schematic port so the label remains visually connected.
- Preserve explicit display labels by not renaming an existing nearby label when its text differs from the fallback label text.

Fixed repro132: https://github.com/tscircuit/core/pull/2501/changes#diff-08a18e894b09a4fc83026c2efefe3ba77a7be63ac6dcf90a0ce6fdffc8668efd

title: Fix use array centers for QFN JSCAD geometry
description: Fixes the QFN footprint rendering issue by changing object-shaped centers to array-shaped centers for QFN body, pads, and thermal pad geometry.
body: ### Motivation QFN footprints failed in the vanilla JSCAD renderer used by 3d-viewer with: `Error: center must be an array of X, Y and Z values` This happened because QFN cuboids passed object-shaped centers instead of `[x, y, z]` arrays. ### Change - Use array centers for QFN body, pads, and thermal pad geometry. - Add a QFN20 fixture. - Add snapshot coverage for QFN20, QFN32, and QFN56 with thermal pad params. [REPRO](https://tscircuit.com/editor?#data:application/gzip;base64,)

title: Fix publish workflow auto-merge fallback
description: Fixes the auto-merge fallback in the publish workflow to handle cases where the pull request is already in a clean state, preventing stale version-bump PRs after a package is published.
body:
## Problem

The `Publish to npm` workflow could fail after the package was already published. The failing step was `Enable auto-merge`, where this command ran against the generated version-bump PR:

```bash
gh pr merge <pr> --auto --squash --delete-branch
```

GitHub sometimes creates that PR in a state where all required checks are already satisfied, so there is nothing for auto-merge to wait on. In real-life terms: the workflow was asking GitHub to "merge this later when it is ready", but GitHub was saying "it is ready right now, so there is no later auto-merge to enable."

The observed error was:

```text
GraphQL: Pull request Pull request is in clean status (enablePullRequestAutoMerge)
```

That left version-bump PRs open and stale even though the release itself had completed.

## Fix

The workflow still tries auto-merge first. If GitHub returns the known clean-status `enablePullRequestAutoMerge` error, it falls back to a direct squash merge. Other GitHub CLI errors still fail the job.

## Validation

- Parsed the workflow YAML.
- Checked the edited shell block with `bash -n`.
- Simulated the clean-status fallback and unrelated-error paths.
- Ran `bun run format`; it failed on existing `.vendor/rectdiff` Biome config incompatibilities and reported no fixes applied.

title: Fill rectdiff gaps across multiple passes using maxGapFillPasses
description: Run gap filling across repeated findexpand passes until a pass adds no nodes, capping the loop at 4 total passes and updating SVG snapshots accordingly.
body:
## Summary

- Run gap filling across repeated find/expand passes until a pass adds no nodes.
- Cap the gap-fill loop at 4 total passes.
- Keep expanded-node visualization across all passes.
- Update SVG snapshots produced by the multi-pass gap fill.

## Root Cause

The previous gap fill only expanded once. Small corner-overlap gaps could require newly expanded nodes to participate in a later find/expand pass, leaving visible holes after the first pass.

Repro that got fixed
https://github.com/tscircuit/rectdiff/pull/133/changes#diff-5c564705806d916d0571b38e7be681977131bc6048582367372e594702516047

title: Introduce Hosted 3D PCB Previews Generated Directly from Circuit JSON Jobs
description: Upgrades job previews from locally rendered PCB SVGs to hosted 3D PCB PNG previews generated from each jobs Circuit JSON.
body:
Upgrades job previews from locally rendered PCB SVGs to hosted 3D PCB PNG previews generated from each job’s Circuit JSON.

This PR includes:

* Circuit JSON to tscircuit JSX conversion for preview generation
* Hosted 3D PNG preview URLs via `createSvgUrl`
* Board-size extraction from Circuit JSON metadata
* Safe component-name generation from job names
* Updated preview loading and error states
* Fabrication page copy updated from SVG preview to 3D PNG preview
* Dependency updates for snippet URL generation and Circuit JSON conversion

This makes fabrication jobs easier to inspect visually by replacing flat SVG previews with richer generated 3D PCB renderings.

* Generic HTTP client with JSON request/response handling

* Typed fabrication job lifecycle APIs (create, get, list, next stage)

* Vite environment type definitions for configuration support

This establishes a centralized client SDK that standardizes communication with the fake fabrication server while providing a scalable foundation for future fabrication workflow integration.

title: Fix: tsci dev uses the project-local tscircuit version when available
description: tsci dev now renders the preview with the tscircuit version installed in the project, instead of always using the runframe baked into the CLI.
body:
`tsci dev` now renders the preview with the `tscircuit` version installed in the project, instead of always using the runframe baked into the CLI.

Previously the dev server served its own bundled `@tscircuit/runframe/standalone` (pinned to the CLI's devDependency, with eval fetched from the CDN at runtime) for every project — so a project pinning a specific `tscircuit` version was ignored.

## How

The `/standalone.min.js` route in `lib/server/createHttpServer.ts` now resolves `tscircuit` from the project dir (`createRequire(projectDir/package.json)`) and, if found, serves that package's `dist/browser.min.js` — the self-contained runframe + eval bundle with the matching `@tscircuit/core`/`@tscircuit/eval`  baked in.

Resolution order:
1. `RUNFRAME_STANDALONE_FILE_PATH` env override (unchanged)
2. project-local `tscircuit/dist/browser.min.js` (new)
3. the runframe bundled into the CLI (existing fallback)

No changes needed in the `tscircuit` or `runframe` repos — `tscircuit` already ships the bundle; the CLI just wasn't looking for it.

title: Prevent CDN fallback in production by preloading bundled ngspice
description: Fixes a production reliability bug in svg.tscircuit.com where server-side circuit evaluation could fall back to CDN-loaded dependencies instead of using bundled runtime packages.
body: ## Summary This PR fixes a production reliability bug in `svg.tscircuit.com` where server-side circuit evaluation could fall back to CDN-loaded dependencies instead of using bundled runtime packages. ## Result ## What changed - preload a shared local `ngspice` engine at server startup - inject the preloaded engine into the eval platform config - disable `CircuitRunner` CDN fallback so production only uses packaged dependencies - add an explicit runtime dependency for `@tscircuit/ngspice-spice-engine` - remove the remaining `fflate` CDN import from the generated index page ## Why this matters `svg.tscircuit.com` is a deployed server and should not depend on CDN resolution at request time. Before this change, eval could silently fall back to remote loading if a dependency was not resolved locally. That makes production behavior less deterministic and can introduce cold-start latency or deployment-time breakage. This change makes the runtime behavior explicit and local-only: - `ngspice` is bundled and warmed ahead of first simulation use - missing packaged dependencies now fail fast instead of silently loading from CDN - the generated page no longer references a browser CDN import ## Bug reproduction and fix This PR addresses a real server/runtime bug pattern: - reproduce path: simulation and generated URL flows exercise the server eval path that previously allowed CDN fallback - fix: preload the local engine and force `disableCdnLoading` in the server runner ## Verification Ran: ```bash bun test tests/schematic-simulation-svg.test.ts tests/generate-urls.test.ts tests/health.test.ts ``` Passed: - schematic simulation svg conversion - POST `/generate_urls` returns HTML without re-reading the body - GET `/health` ## Impact This is a subtle but important server/runtime fix focused on: - production reliability - deterministic dependency loading - lower first-request simulation overhead - better deployment correctness for packaged environments

title: Fix circuit-json-to-3d-png render option forwarding for size, background, grid, and supersampling
description: Fixes the issue where render options for width, height, background color, grid visibility, and supersampling were not forwarded to the underlying renderer, resulting in all outputs defaulting to 800x600 resolution with default settings.
body:
## Summary

`renderCircuitJsonTo3dPng` only forwarded **camera** options to poppygl's `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB`. The underlying renderer already supports `width`, `height`, `backgroundColor`, `grid`, and `supersampling
`, but `renderCircuitJsonTo3dPng` neither exposed nor passed them through — so callers had **no way** to control output resolution, background, the infinite grid, or anti-aliasing, and any such option was
silently dropped.

## The bug

- `RenderCircuitJsonTo3dPngOptions` did not declare these fields, and even at runtime the values never reached the renderer.
- Every render therefore came out at poppygl's default 800×600 with the default background/grid, **regardless of the options passed in**.

## Fix

- Extend `RenderCircuitJsonTo3dPngOptions` with `width`, `height`, `backgroundColor`, `showInfiniteGrid`, and `supersampling`.
- Forward each to `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` **only when provided**, so existing defaults are preserved for callers that omit them.
- `backgroundColor` is forwarded using an explicit `undefined` check so `null` (transparent) stays a valid, distinct value rather than being treated as "unset".
- `showInfiniteGrid: true` maps to the renderer's infinite-grid configuration.

## Reproduction + fix in the same PR

Added `tests/render-circuit-json-to-3d-png-render-options.test.ts`, which renders the basic-board fixture with explicit options and decodes the PNG IHDR header to assert the actual output dimensions.

These tests **fail against the previous behavior** (options ignored → output stays at the 800×600 default) and **pass with this fix**, covering the size, background + grid, and supersampling paths.

title: Align same-side passive groups into a row (ParallelAlignedPassiveSolver)
description: Fixes layout scattering of same-side passive components by aligning them into a clean, evenly-spaced row adjacent to the main chip.
body:
## What & why

`calculate-packing` (the packer behind `SingleInnerPartitionPackingSolver`) has
no concept of pin sides — it only minimises pad-to-pad distance. So when several
2-pin passives all hang off one side of a chip, it places them one at a time and
**scatters** them. This is the bad placement captured by the repros added in
#143 (bq24074 bottom programming resistors R1/R2/R3) and #144 (right-side
EN2/EN1/TMR resistors). This PR fixes those.

Following the maintainer's guidance — *"after splitting into partitions, choose
based on partition contents which algorithm to run"* — we keep the existing
partitioning and packer untouched and add a **content-aware layout path** for the
one shape the generic packer handles badly.

## How it works

Three pieces, all inside `PackInnerPartitionsSolver`:

1. **`findSameSidePassiveGroups()`** — a pure detector. It finds "same-side
   passive groups": ≥3 two-pin passives that each connect by a single strong
   pin-to-pin link to the **same side of the same chip** and share a common net
   on their other pin (e.g. R1/R2/R3 → U1's bottom ITERM/ILIM/ISET, all to GND).
   This mirrors how `IdentifyDecouplingCapsSolver` groups caps around a
   `mainChip`, and reuses the same vocabulary (`mainChipId`, `passiveChipIds`).

2. **`ParallelAlignedPassiveSolver`** — the alternate layout solver. It runs the
   normal packer first, then **re-flows only the detected group(s)** into a clean,
   evenly-spaced horizontal row just outside the main-chip edge (pushed
   left/right/up/down depending on which edge the group attaches to), ordered by
   the connecting main-chip pin. Every other component stays exactly where the
   packer put it, and each passive keeps its fixed (core-supplied) rotation.

3. **Dispatch** — `PackInnerPartitionsSolver` selects a solver per partition from
   an extensible strategy registry, `PARTITION_SOLVER_STRATEGIES`. Each entry
   pairs an `appliesTo(partition)` predicate with a solver class; strategies are
   tried in order and a partition matched by none falls back to the generic
   `SingleInnerPartitionPackingSolver`. Adding a future partition solver is a
   one-line append — no other dispatch code changes, and no conditional branching.

## Guarantees

- **Bounds-aware:** clearance is measured against pin-inclusive bounding boxes
  (so the row clears pins that protrude past a chip body), and the row slides
  along its axis to keep `chipGap` from every neighbouring component.
- **Never worse:** if a group can't be cleared by sliding, it is left at its
  packed position; partitions with no group use the generic packer untouched.

## Blast radius & testing

Only partitions containing a same-side passive group take the new path. Three
snapshots improve and are regenerated:
- `repro-bq24074-bottom-resistors` — R1/R2/R3 now form an even row below U1.
- `repro-bq24074-right-resistors` — now a clean horizontal row beside U1.
- `repro-bq24074-battery-charger` — the full board's bottom resistors row-align.

`bun test`: **38 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail**. No other snapshots change.

## Files

- **New** `lib/solvers/PackInnerPartitionsSolver/findSameSidePassiveGroups.ts` — detector
- **New** `lib/solvers/PackInnerPartitionsSolver/ParallelAlignedPassiveSolver.ts` — alternate solver
- **New** `lib/utils/rotatePinOffset.ts` — shared 0/90/180/270 pin-offset + size rotation
- **Edit** `lib/solvers/PackInnerPartitionsSolver/PackInnerPartitionsSolver.ts` — strategy-registry dispatch
- Geometry (bounds / centre / distance) uses the existing `@tscircuit/math-utils` dependency.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add fast-footprint-compare app for strict Footprinter/JLCPCB comparison
description: This PR turns fast-footprint-compare from the initial placeholder repo into a working comparison tool for validating a footprinter string against the JLCPCBEasyEDA footprint for an exact JLC part number.
body:
## Summary

This PR turns `fast-footprint-compare` from the initial placeholder repo into a working comparison tool for validating a `footprinter` string against the JLCPCB/EasyEDA footprint for an exact JLC part number.

## What changed

- added the React + Vite frontend for entering a footprinter string and JLCPCB part number
- added strict input validation:
  - footprinter input must build directly with `@tscircuit/footprinter`

title: Add .kicad_mod footprint export support and snapshot coverage
description: Add support for exporting KiCad footprints as .kicad_mod files, including snapshot coverage and test validation for generated outputs.
body:
Issue #327 
## Summary

- add `CircuitJsonToKicadModConverter` for exporting generated KiCad footprints as `.kicad_mod`
- export the new converter from the public API
- add `.kicad_mod` snapshot support in `tests/fixtures/take-kicad-snapshot.ts` by wrapping the footprint in a temporary PCB for rendering
- add motor-controller test coverage for generated `.kicad_mod` output and snapshot validation
- document `.kicad_mod` usage in the README

## Notes

- `kicad_pcb` and `.kicad_mod` are not the same kind of KiCad file
- PCB files can contain traces, vias, zones, and board-level graphics
- footprint files mainly contain footprint primitives such as `fpPads`, `fpLines`, `fpPolys`, `fpTexts`, and models
- because of that, a full circuit JSON cannot be dumped 1:1 into a single `.kicad_mod` the same way it can be converted into `.kicad_pcb`
- for multi-footprint circuit JSON inputs, the current flow validates a selected generated footprint instead of flattening the entire circuit into one footprint file
- this PR does not include any Bun crash fix; it only adds `.kicad_mod` conversion and test support

title: Add multi-file WorkspaceCodeEditor with cross-file type-checking, go-to-definition, and per-file editor state
description: Adds a WorkspaceCodeEditor that upgrades the editor from single-file to a full multi-file workspace, enabling cross-file type-checking, go-to-definition functionality, and maintaining per-file editor state.
body:
## Summary

Adds a `WorkspaceCodeEditor` that upgrades the editor from single-file to a full multi-file workspace — the same editing experience tscircuit.com users get when a package spans several `.tsx`/`.ts`/`.json` files. Each file is backed by its own Monaco model, so the TypeScript language service now resolves cross-file imports, reports diagnostics project-wide, and supports go-to-definition that jumps between files instead of failing silently.

The existing single-file `CodeEditor` is preserved, and the shared Monaco setup is factored out so both components stay in sync.

## Why

The previous `CodeEditor` only ever held one file in memory. For real tscircuit projects (a board `index.tsx` importing chip components and `manual-edits.json`), that meant no cross-file autocomplete, broken "go to definition" across files, and lost cursor/scroll position when switching files. This is a significant editing-UX upgrade for the web editor.

## What's new

- **Multi-model workspace manager** (`MonacoWorkspaceModelManager`) — keeps one live Monaco model per file and syncs creates/updates/renames/deletes, so the TS language service type-checks across the whole project, not just the open file.
- **Cross-file go-to-definition** — registers a Monaco editor opener that routes navigation through `onFileSelect`, so jumping to a symbol in another workspace file switches the active file and reveals the line.
- **Per-file editor state** — cursor position and scroll are saved/restored per file when switching tabs.
- **File management** — create / rename / delete with a sidebar, plus a streaming preview mode, binary-file handling, and read-only/saving states.
- **Debounced tscircuit type acquisition** for the active file.
- **`useWorkspaceFiles`** — an in-memory controller you can spread straight into the component (`<WorkspaceCodeEditor {...controller} />`).

Props are kept structurally compatible with tscircuit.com's `useFileManagement` hook, so this can drop in for the site's current editor.

## Public API

New exports from the package entrypoint:

- `WorkspaceCodeEditor` + types (`EditorFile`, `WorkspaceCodeEditorProps`, file-op prop/result types)
- `useWorkspaceFiles`, `useMonacoReady`, `useTscircuitTypeAcquisition`
- `MonacoWorkspaceModelManager` / `createMonacoWorkspaceModelManager`

## Structure

Source is reorganized into `components/`, `hooks/`, and `monaco/` so the shared Monaco/Shiki/TypeScript bootstrap (`ensureMonacoConfigured`) is owned in one place and consumed by both editors via `useMonacoReady`.

## Try it

`examples/TscircuitWorkspace.fixture.tsx` boots a 3-file board project (`index.tsx` importing `MPL3115A2R1.tsx` and `manual-edits.json`) — open it in Cosmos to see cross-file autocomplete and go-to-definition working between files.

title: Add Shiki syntax highlighting and centralize Monaco editor setup
description: Adds Shiki-based syntax highlighting for TSX and centralizes the Monaco editor setup, improving the default editing experience and removing unsupported theme API.
body:
## Summary

This PR upgrades the editor integration by adding Shiki-based syntax highlighting, centralizing Monaco setup, and aligning the `CodeEditor` API with the supported configuration.

## Changes

- add Shiki + Monaco integration for TSX syntax highlighting
- add a custom Monaco tokens provider backed by Shiki tokenization
- move Monaco loader/highlighter setup into `src/monacoSetup.ts`
- extract shared editor theme and default editor options into `src/editorTheme.ts`
- update Monaco TypeScript defaults for TSX editing and diagnostics
- remove the unsupported `theme` prop from `CodeEditor`
- render `CodeEditor` only after Monaco setup completes
- update the example fixture styling and editor options
- add required dependencies and lockfile updates

## Why

Before this change, Monaco configuration lived directly in the editor component and syntax highlighting relied on Monaco defaults. This branch introduces a dedicated setup path for Monaco + Shiki, improves the default editing experience, and removes a public `theme` API that was broader than the actual supported setup.

## Validation

- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`

* Reusable transform APIs for the jig, feeder wheels, PCB, and complete bench

* Automatic PCB feed motion derived from feeder wheel rotation

This establishes a reusable kinematics layer for the laser fabrication bench, making mechanical behavior deterministic, testable, and reusable across rendering, simulation, and future motion-planning workflows.

title: feat(sch): add convertCircuitJsonToStackedSchematicSheetsSvg for multi-sheet schematics
description: Adds convertCircuitJsonToStackedSchematicSheetsSvg to render all schematic sheets stacked vertically into a single SVG for easier debugging of multi-sheet designs.
body:
`convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSvg` only renders one schematic sheet at a time. This adds `convertCircuitJsonToStackedSchematicSheetsSvg`, which renders **every** sheet stacked vertically into a single SVG — one labeled panel per sheet — so you can eyeball all sheets at once. Primarily a debugging aid for multi-sheet designs.

## Behavior
- Sheets are ordered by `sheet_index` ascending (top → bottom), full width.
- Each panel is the regular single-sheet render of that sheet (sheet border included), so panels look identical to the normal schematic view.
- Each panel shows the sheet's `display_name` (falling back to `name`, then `Sheet N`) in a band above the frame, left-aligned to the frame's top-left corner.
- Panel height defaults to the A4 sheet aspect ratio (297:210) when `height` is omitted, so panels fill the width without letterboxing.
- Falls back to `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSvg` when there are fewer than 2 sheets.

## Implementation notes

- Follows the existing composite-SVG pattern from `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSimulationSvg` (render sub-SVGs → `parseSync` → compose).
- Extracted the shared composition helpers (`ensureElementNode`, `cloneSvgObject`, `translateNestedSvg`, `formatNumber`) into `lib/utils/svg-object-utils.ts` and refactored the simulation converter to use them. The simulation refactor is behavior-identical (its snapshot is unchanged).

title: fix: handle non-rotated pill pads in trace clearance/overlap checks
description: Fixes clearance violation detection for non-rotated pill pads in trace clearance and overlap checks by routing them through the existing pill-geometry path, ensuring accurate clearance reporting.
body:
The trace-clearance and trace-overlap DRC checks only special-cased `rotated_pill` pads, so a plain `pill` pad fell through to axis-aligned **bounding-box** geometry — whose corners overstate the rounded pad by ~0.2mm. A trace routed cleanly near a pill pad's corner was wrongly flagged as a clearance violation / "accidental contact".
Fix: route plain `pill` pads through the existing pill-geometry path (`getSegmentToPillClearance` / `isPillPad`), exactly like `rotated_pill`.
Added regression tests: the rounded-corner false positive is ignored, and a real violation near the flat edge is still reported.

Closes #162

title: Fix editor auth dead-end and unify login redirect handling
description: Fixes an auth dead-end in the web editor by making the Not logged in, cant save warning clickable, directing users to the login page and preserving their current location after login.
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes an auth dead-end in the web editor and unifies login redirect handling across the UI.

Previously, unauthenticated users in the editor saw `Not logged in, can't save`, but that warning was not actionable. On smaller screens, the visible warning could be easier to find than the actual login entry point, leaving users blocked from saving without a clear next step.

This change makes the editor warning directly open the login page and centralizes login redirect path generation so auth entry points consistently preserve the current in-app location.

## Changes

- make the `Not logged in, can't save` warning in the editor clickable
- send users from the editor warning directly to `/login`
- preserve the current path, query string, and hash in the login redirect target
- add shared redirect helpers in `src/lib/utils/handle-redirect.ts`
- reuse the shared login redirect path in:
  - `EditorNav`
  - `HeaderLogin`
  - the 401 auth toast flow in `use-axios`

## Why this matters

- removes a dead-end in the editor save flow
- improves login discoverability, especially on mobile
- keeps users on the same editor context after signing in
- reduces duplicated auth redirect logic across the app

## Root cause

Login redirect path generation was duplicated across multiple auth entry points, and the editor's unauthenticated save warning exposed the blocked state without providing a direct recovery path.

## Validation

- `bun run typecheck`

title: fix: roll over to next SI prefix when rounding rounds up to 1000
description: Fixes the SI unit formatting to roll over to the next prefix when rounding results in a value of 1000, preventing exponential notation for values like 999.5 and 999999.
body:
`formatSiUnit` picks the SI prefix from the raw value before `toPrecision(3)` rounds it, so inputs near a band's top (e.g. 999.5, 999999) round up to 1000 and render as exponential notation like "1.00e+3" instead of "1k"/"1M".
Fix: after rounding, roll over to the next-larger prefix.
Added a regression test.

Closes #9

title: test: reproduce formatSiUnit exponential-notation bug near band boundaries
description: Reproduces the exponential-notation bug in formatSiUnit when values are near SI band boundaries, ensuring that the prefix rollover is correctly handled in future fixes.
body:
Per review feedback on #10 — a standalone test reproducing #9.

`formatSiUnit` chooses the SI prefix from the raw value **before** the existing 3-sig-fig rounding runs. So a value just under a band's top (≥ 999.5 × prefix) gets rounded up to `1000` by `toPrecision(3)` — which JS serializes as `"1.00e+3"` — and nothing rolls over to the next prefix:

```
formatSiUnit(999.5)   // "1.00e+3"   (expected "1k")
formatSiUnit(999999)  // "1.00e+3k"  (expected "1M")
```

**This test is expected to fail on `main`** — that failure *is* the repro. The rounding itself is intended/pre-existing behavior (the `"handles numbers needing rounding"` test asserts `1234 → "1.23k"`); the bug is the missing prefix rollover, not the rounding.

The fix lives in #10. Refs #9, #10.

body:
## Summary

This PR adds package-level chip exports alongside the existing subcircuit exports and updates the README to document them clearly.

### What changed

- added short-name chip exports from the package entrypoint
  - examples: `BQ24074`, `BQ25895`, `INA237`, `TPS7A02`
- kept the underlying MPN-specific chip definitions available as direct exports
  - example: `BQ24074RGTR`
- added chip export helpers/types:
  - `TiChipComponents`
  - `TiChipName`
  - `TiChipComponent`
- updated the README with:
  - raw chip usage example
  - exported chip list
  - short-name to current chip-definition mapping
  - clarification that subcircuits are still the higher-level default

## Why

We currently have chips in `tscircuit/ti` that come from JLCPCB-backed MPN definitions, but they were only exposed/documented by those MPN names. This makes the package API harder to discover and inconsistent with the preferred short-name naming approach.

This PR makes the chip API easier to use while preserving access to the exact underlying MPN-specific definitions.

title: Fix Monaco workspace initialization and selected-file preview regressions in WorkspaceCodeEditor
description: Fixes regressions in the WorkspaceCodeEditor that caused the editor to remain stuck on loading and allowed non-runnable helper files to be selected as the preview.
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two regressions in the workspace editing and preview flow:

1. `WorkspaceCodeEditor` could remain stuck on `Loading editor...` even after the priority file was fetched.
2. The runframe preview could switch to non-runnable helper files when the selected file was not a real entrypoint.

It also strengthens Monaco workspace model initialization so cross-file TypeScript state is synchronized before the editor is shown.

## What Changed

- Added explicit workspace model readiness tracking in `WorkspaceCodeEditor`.
- Switched initial workspace model sync to `useLayoutEffect` so Monaco models are populated before the editor renders.
- Added a post-mount re-sync to trigger a clean TypeScript pass once the editor is mounted.
- Fixed the `isPriorityFileFetched` compatibility gate so `true` means the priority file is ready instead of blocking editor mount.
- Tightened tscircuit entrypoint resolution so selected files only override the preview when they look like a real default-exported entry component.
- Prevented named-export helper files from hijacking the live preview.
- Updated Monaco workspace URIs to use `monaco.Uri.file(...)` for file-backed workspace models.
- Expanded the runframe fixture to exercise a multi-file board setup with imported components and manual edits.

## User Impact

Before this change:
- Some hosts could leave the editor permanently stuck in a loading state.
- Monaco could initialize before the full workspace model graph was ready.
- Selecting a helper TSX file could redirect the preview away from the actual runnable board entrypoint.

After this change:
- The editor mounts once the workspace and priority file are actually ready.
- Cross-file Monaco state is synchronized earlier and refreshed after mount.
- The preview stays on a runnable board entrypoint unless the selected file is a valid default-exported entry file.

## Why This Matters

This is a user-facing workspace UX fix. It improves editor readiness, stabilizes cross-file Monaco behavior, and prevents incorrect preview switching during normal file navigation in multi-file tscircuit projects.

## Validation

- `bun run typecheck`
